> how to export:
  1. Wait 2–3s for Google Fonts to load, then click download all 9 slides.
  2. All images use relative paths. Serve day-49/ over http://localhost for export.
  3. Upload all 9 slides in order to Instagram.

~/amazon-clone $ ls components
AMAZON CLONE
what i'm
building
/login
Login PageEmail + password form using useRef — no state, no re-renders
Portal
Modal via React PortalRenders into #modal-root-div — outside the React root
<>
JSX FragmentsReplaced wrapper divs with <>...</> in App.jsx
Effect
useEffect HookSide effects — data fetching, subscriptions, DOM mutations
concepts: fragments · portals · refs · effects
03 / 09
~/amazon-clone $ git log --today
DAY 49
what i've
learnt
1
JSX FragmentsJSX needs one root — use <></> instead of an extra div
2
React PortalsRender outside #root into any DOM node — great for modals
3
useRef()Read DOM values directly — no state, no re-renders on change
4
Controlled vs UncontrolledState = controlled · ref = uncontrolled (DOM manages value)
5
useEffect()Runs side effects after render — pass [] to run once on mount
hooks: useRef · useEffect · useState
04 / 09
~/amazon-clone $ App.jsx
JSX
react
fragments
// JSX must return one root element // ❌ Error — adjacent elements return ( <h1>Title</h1> <p>Text</p> // SyntaxError ) // ✅ React.Fragment — no extra DOM node return ( <React.Fragment> <h1>Title</h1> <p>Text</p> </React.Fragment> ) // ✅ Shorthand <> — same thing return ( <><h1>Title</h1><p>Text</p></> )
tip: no extra div → cleaner DOM tree
05 / 09
~/amazon-clone $ Login.jsx
REACT PORTAL
react
portals
// index.html — add sibling div <div id="root"></div> <div id="modal-root-div"></div> // Login.jsx — render Modal outside root import ReactDom from "react-dom"; const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false); {isOpen && ReactDom.createPortal( <Modal setIsOpen={setIsOpen} />, document.getElementById("modal-root-div") )}
note: modal renders outside React root — no z-index issues
06 / 09
~/amazon-clone $ Login.jsx
REACT
useRef
inputs
import { useRef } from "react"; const Login = () => { const emailRef = useRef(); const passwordRef = useRef(); const signIn = (e) => { e.preventDefault(); const email = emailRef.current.value; }; return ( <input type="email" ref={emailRef} /> // no value={} onChange={} = uncontrolled ); };
uncontrolled: DOM holds value · no re-render on change
07 / 09
~/amazon-clone $ Home.jsx
REACT HOOKS
use
effect
import { useState, useEffect } from "react"; const App = () => { const [data, setData] = useState(null); useEffect(() => { // runs after every render fetch("/api/products") .then(r => r.json()) .then(d => setData(d)); }, []); // [] = run once on mount return <div>{data}</div>; };
tip: remove [] to re-run on every render
08 / 09
